title: Gold Wing

{{Quote|We're on our way. Red Group, Gold Group, all fighters follow me.|Lando Calrissian, leading Gold Wing and others in an attack on the Death Star|<ref name="ROTJ">{{Film|VI}}</ref>}}
'''Gold Wing''' was the [[Callsign/Legends|call sign]] of a [[Pilot/Legends|member]] of [[Gold Squadron (Rebel Alliance)/Legends|Gold Squadron]], a [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]] [[Starfighter/Legends|starfighter]] [[Squadron/Legends|squadron]], during the [[Battle of Endor/Legends|Battle of Endor]] in [[4 ABY/Legends|4 ABY]]. Gold Wing was one of many pilots who followed [[Gold Leader/Legends|Gold Leader]] [[Lando Calrissian/Legends|Lando Calrissian]], flying the [[YT-1300 light freighter/Legends|light freighter]] [[Millennium Falcon/Legends|''Millennium Falcon'']], into the superstructure of the incomplete [[Death Star II/Legends|second Death Star]] near the climax of the battle. The pilot remained with Calrissian and [[Red Leader/Legends|Red Leader]] [[Wedge Antilles/Legends|Wedge Antilles]] as they approached the [[Battle station/Legends|battle station's]] [[Reactor/Legends|reactor core]], but Gold Wing was shot down by an [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] [[TIE/ln space superiority starfighter/Legends|TIE fighter]] before they could reach their target.

==Biography==
[[File:Insider221-Subscribercover-cropped.png|thumb|250px|Gold Wing joined other Rebel pilots in an attack on the second Death Star's reactor.]]
Though the term "Gold Wing" could refer to the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance's]] [[Gold Squadron (Rebel Alliance)/Legends|Gold Squadron]],<ref name="CSWE">''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]''</ref> the designation was also the [[Callsign/Legends|call sign]] of an individual [[Pilot/Legends|pilot]] who served with the [[Squadron/Legends|squadron]].<ref name="ROTJ novel" /> In [[4 ABY/Legends|4 ABY]],<ref name="Date" /> after learning that the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] was constructing a [[Death Star II/Legends|second Death Star]] in the [[Endor system/Legends|Endor system]], the Rebel Alliance decided to launch an [[Battle of Endor/Legends|attack]] on the incomplete [[Battle station/Legends|battle station]]. A [[Endor strike team/Legends|commando team]] was dispatched to the [[Endor/Legends|Forest Moon of Endor]] to destroy the [[Deflector shield generator/Legends|shield generator]] protecting the [[Death Star/Legends|Death Star]]; with the [[Deflector shield/Legends|deflector shield]] out of the way, the [[Alliance Fleet/Legends|Alliance Fleet]], under the command of [[Mon Calamari/Legends|Mon Calamari]] [[Admiral/Legends|Admiral]] [[Gial Ackbar/Legends|Ackbar]], could arrive in-[[Star system/Legends|system]] to destroy the station. However, the Alliance had fallen into a trap orchestrated by [[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Emperor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine]]; when the Alliance Fleet arrived, they were trapped between an Imperial [[Fleet/Legends|fleet]] and a shielded and operational Death Star. As the battle raged between the opposing armadas, the strike team succeeded in destroying the shield generator, leaving the Death Star vulnerable to attack.<ref name="ROTJ" />

[[General/Legends|General]] [[Lando Calrissian/Legends|Lando Calrissian]], flying the [[YT-1300 light freighter/Legends|freighter]] [[Millennium Falcon/Legends|''Millennium Falcon'']] as [[Gold Leader/Legends|Gold Leader]], used the opportunity to lead several Rebel starfighters—including Gold Wing—into the battlestation's superstructure to destroy the Death Star's main [[Reactor/Legends|reactor]]. Racing through the battlestation's interior, the Rebels contended with numerous obstacles and pursuing Imperial [[TIE/ln space superiority starfighter/Legends|TIE fighters]]. Several Alliance starfighters broke off and doubled back to the Death Star's surface, luring away some of the [[TIE series/Legends|TIE]] fighters and leaving Calrissian, [[Red Leader/Legends|Red Leader]] [[Wedge Antilles/Legends|Wedge Antilles]], and Gold Wing to destroy the reactor. Gold Wing fell in behind the other two as the trio of [[Starship/Legends|ships]] homed in on their target, but this move left the pilot vulnerable—Gold Wing was shot down by one of the remaining Imperials. Calrissian and Antilles managed to take out the main reactor, creating a chain reaction that destroyed the Death Star.<ref name="ROTJ novel" />

==Behind the scenes==
The unidentified pilot known only as "Gold Wing" briefly appeared in [[James Kahn]]'s [[1983]] [[Star Wars: Return of the Jedi (novelization)|novelization]] of the [[original trilogy]] film [[Star Wars: Episode VI Return of the Jedi|''Star Wars'': Episode VI ''Return of the Jedi'']].<ref name="ROTJ novel" />
